summary: Soundboard capture of an early 1966 show with vibrant psychedelic R&B, great song selection and strong playing despite some channel drop‑outs and vinyl‑like noise.
review: The performance is celebrated for its raw energy – standout numbers like Viola Lee Blues, Big Boy Pete, Early Morning Rain and Same Thing showcase the band's early blues/folk blend and a superb closing jam. The recording is a soundboard source but suffers from channel drop‑outs, occasional muddiness and a vinyl‑style hiss that keep it from being pristine, yet most listeners find it listenable and historically valuable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers award 4–5 stars and praise the musical performance; technical flaws are noted but do not dominate, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Drop‑out on the right channel during Viola Lee Blues, occasional channel imbalance and vinyl‑like hiss; overall representation is decent but not pristine.

standout_songs: I Just Want To Make Love To You
See That My Grave Is Kept Clean
show_quality.setlist_flow: A loose early‑Dead set that swings between blues, folk and psychedelic R&B; the third set builds to an energetic closing jam that leaves a strong impression.

band_member_comments.Drums: Kreutzmann starts off on a bebop‑style note and his drumming is highlighted as a highlight of the show.
